pi_items.source: Confirmed in setup_task.sh line 31 and export_result.sh line 12
Confirmed in task.json description section lines 19-23 and verified in export_result.sh file checks

Web search confirmed these are actual jstat -gcutil output column headers. S0=survivor space 0, S1=survivor space 1, E=eden space, O=old generation, M=metaspace, CCS=compressed class space, YGC=young generation GC count, YGCT=young generation GC time
Web search confirmed these patterns appear in jstack output. jstack provides thread state information with 'java.lang.Thread.State', stack traces with 'at ', and native thread IDs with 'nid='
Web search confirmed jcmd VM.flags outputs -XX configuration flags, and VM.system_properties outputs java.vm.* and java.user.dir properties

Web search confirmed these strings appear in jmap -heap output. MinHeapFreeRatio and MaxHeapFreeRatio control heap shrinkage, Metaspace is Java 8+ class metadata storage, class space is compressed class space
These are standard units and terminology for memory reporting in JVM diagnostics
Web search confirmed these are the four main Java thread states that appear in jstack output
All are valid JVM tuning parameters and recommendations. -Xmx is max heap, -Xms is initial heap, G1GC and ParallelGC are garbage collector algorithms
setup_openice.sh line 10 sets JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64, and setup_task.sh line 13 confirms same path

setup_task.sh searches for 'demo-apps' process, setup_openice.sh launches OpenICE via gradlew, repository path /opt/openice/mdpnp confirms mdpnp GitHub project. OpenICE is an open-source integrated clinical environment platform
setup_task.sh lines 18-28 verify all five tools are available in JDK 17, described in Oracle JDK 17 documentation

pi_items.verified_value: File timestamp verification to ensure files created during task execution
pi_items.source: export_result.sh lines 31-35 check file modification times against task start time (recorded in /tmp/task_start_time.txt at line 9 of setup_task.sh)

privileged_info_summary: The JVM Runtime Forensics task runs on a Linux system with JDK 17 (OpenJDK) and targets the OpenICE medical device simulator application. The task requires agents to use five standard JVM diagnostic tools (jps, jstack, jstat, jcmd, jmap) to capture four diagnostic files (heap_stats.txt, thread_dump.txt, jvm_config.txt, heap_summary.txt) and produce a performance_report.txt with PID, heap utilization metrics, thread state analysis, and JVM tuning recommendations. Diagnostic file validation uses specific pattern matching: heap statistics show GC columns (S0, S1, E, O, M, CCS, YGC, YGCT), thread dumps contain java.lang.Thread.State and native IDs (nid=), JVM config shows -XX flags and java.vm properties, and heap summary displays MinHeapFreeRatio, MaxHeapFreeRatio, and Metaspace information. Anti-gaming verification checks that all files are created during task execution using modification timestamps. The task has a 600-second timeout and requires the target Java process to remain running after diagnostics.
